City Council Approves New Park Funding

By Priya Mehta | June 15, 2026

The city council voted 7-2 on Tuesday night to approve $4.2 million in funding for the long-delayed riverside park renovation project, ending nearly three years of budget negotiations.

Council member Diego Alvarez, who championed the proposal, called the vote "a turning point for the east side of the city." The funding will cover new walking trails, a renovated boathouse, and accessibility upgrades throughout the park grounds.

What happens next

Construction is expected to begin in early autumn, with the first phase -- trail resurfacing and lighting -- slated for completion by next spring. City planners say the full renovation could take up to eighteen months.

  • Phase 1: Trail resurfacing and lighting
  • Phase 2: Boathouse renovation
  • Phase 3: Accessibility upgrades and new playground equipment
"This has been a long time coming for residents on the east side," said Council member Alvarez.

Residents can submit feedback on the design plans at upcoming community meetings scheduled throughout July.
